strategy_principles_add: Statement of Claim should be restructured to LEAD with MNDA claims. Prepare anti-stay arguments using the 6 arguments outlined in Part 7.2. The two-front advantage (Alberta court for MNDA claims + Nigerian arbitration for SDA claims) maximizes litigation cost pressure on TON.

claims_map.C1_bad_faith_termination: SDA claim — subject to potential Nigerian arbitration stay. Lower priority in Alberta filing.
claims_map.C2_axxela_circumvention: MNDA claim — exclusive Alberta court jurisdiction confirmed. IMMUNE to arbitration stay. This is the primary Alberta claim.
claims_map.C3_misrepresentation: MNDA subject matter — captured by 'irrespective of legal theory' language. Alberta court. Forum question RESOLVED.
claims_map.C4_unjust_enrichment: Independent restitutionary claim — arguable whether subject to stay. Strong argument it's independent of both agreements.

suggested_updates.evidence_map: Add MNDA Section 6 (exclusive Alberta jurisdiction), MNDA Section 8 (non-circumvention verbatim), MNDA Section 4 (injunctive relief — contemplates court enforcement), MNDA Section 9 (2-year term expires Jan 10, 2027 — all circumvention within operative period) as critical procedural evidence.
suggested_updates.filing_readiness: Upgrade Alberta confidence level — the MNDA exclusive jurisdiction clause is a procedural asset, not a risk. The mandatory stay risk applies only to SDA-specific claims, not to the core circumvention/confidence claims.
suggested_updates.strategy: Statement of Claim should be restructured to LEAD with MNDA claims. Prepare anti-stay arguments using the 6 arguments outlined in Part 7.2. The two-front advantage (Alberta court for MNDA claims + Nigerian arbitration for SDA claims) maximizes litigation cost pressure on TON.
